Title: UAV-Based Hybrid Fuzzy Inference Framework for Symmetry and Asymmetry in Real-Time Air Quality Monitoring
D1_Target_output: AQI reconstruction and pollutant concentration assessment
D2_Inference_design_summary: Hybrid FIS combining symmetric and asymmetric rules for inverse pollutant reconstruction and AQI estimation in UAV monitoring
Eval_Key_results_summary: Hybrid FIS reduced RMSE by about 30-40% versus baseline methods and improved robustness under asymmetric disturbances in indoor/urban/desert tests
Main_contribution_reported: UAV-based hybrid symmetric/asymmetric fuzzy inference framework for robust real-time air-quality monitoring and inverse AQI reconstruction
Main_limitation_reported: Hardware constraints (payload, endurance, wind sensitivity), sensor nonlinearity/accuracy issues, and communication range limitations; future work includes fuzzy optimization and scalable UAV deployment

Title: ADFIST: Adaptive Dynamic Fuzzy Inference System Tree Driven by Optimized Knowledge Base for Indoor Air Quality Assessment
D1_Env_variables_inputs: NR (focus on PM2.5 time-series and contextual predictors)
D1_Target_output: Indoor PM2.5 level prediction and IAQ assessment
D2_Inference_design_summary: Dynamic fuzzy tree model with adaptive rule/structure updates for PM2.5 prediction
Eval_Key_results_summary: ADFIST reports strong predictive performance and outperforms several baseline/alternative methods on indoor PM2.5 tasks
Eval_Dataset_size_values: Train=2956; Validation=739; GAMS_raw=135100; GAMS_preprocessed=3058; GAMS_train=2446; GAMS_validation=612
Main_contribution_reported: Proposes ADFIST as an adaptive fuzzy tree approach for robust indoor PM2.5 prediction in IoT-enabled IAQ monitoring
Main_limitation_reported: Generalization and broader multi-site validation remain future expansion points (limited deployment scope)

Title: An intelligent IoT fuzzy based approach for automated indoor air quality monitoring
Venue: 2021 29th Mediterranean Conference on Control and Automation, MED 2021
D1_Sampling_frequency_values: Second-scale sampling in reported tests (exact rate NR)
D2_Inference_design_summary: 2 CO inputs (dual MQ-7), 1 AQI output; 6 input MFs per sensor + 6 output MFs; 36 rules
D3_Hardware_and_sensors: CO_Sensor; PM_Sensor; TempHumidity_Sensor; MCU_NodeMCU; RTC_Module; Alarm_Module
Eval_Key_results_summary: Detected dangerous CO event in bathroom (~250 ppm) with alarm; kitchen and room tests mostly good AQI; PM peak around 25 ug/m3 in one room test
Main_contribution_reported: IoT-fuzzy AQM device with BI-CO type-1 FIS, dual-MQ7 calibration strategy, and cloud-connected monitoring workflow
Main_limitation_reported: Future work: year-long data-driven model with ML and Type-2 fuzzy sets

Title: Monitoring of air pollution to establish optimal less polluted path by utilizing wireless sensor network
Venue: Journal of Ambient Intelligence and Humanized Computing
D1_Target_output: AQI severity class and optimal less-polluted path
D2_Inference_design_summary: K-Medoid clustering + ANFIS (5-layer structure) to classify severity into 6 AQI levels
Eval_Key_results_summary: Proposed I-ANFIS and AOMDV-KH reported higher performance than baselines; ANFIS-based approach reports up to ~98.9% accuracy in presented comparisons
Main_contribution_reported: Integrated WSN-based air pollution monitoring and path optimization framework combining preprocessing, I-ANFIS severity analysis, and KHOA-supported multipath routing
Main_limitation_reported: NR (no explicit limitations section; emphasis on reported improvements)

Title: Personalised healthcare model for monitoring and prediction of airpollution: machine learning approach
Venue: Journal of Experimental and Theoretical Artificial Intelligence
Country_or_region: India (framework context); datasets from multiple regions
D1_Env_variables_inputs: NR (core model centered on pollutant concentrations)
D1_Target_output: LoP (Level of Pollutant) and modified AQI (m-AQI); pollutant level prediction
D2_Inference_design_summary: Five-layer ANFIS architecture with DE-based parameter tuning and rule adaptation for pollutant prediction
Eval_Metrics_reported: Temporal_Delay; Accuracy; COD; RMSE; Reliability; Stability_AAS
Eval_Key_results_summary: Reported improvements over baselines/ensembles across four datasets; accuracy up to 98.9%, COD up to 0.95, and reduced RMSE in comparative tables
Main_contribution_reported: IoT-Fog air-quality prediction framework combining LoP/m-AQI formulation with ANFIS-DE optimization and real-time visualization/alerts
Main_limitation_reported: Open challenges noted: network efficiency and human-computer interaction for visualization/services
